statusDefinitions.implemented: The exact bounded scope has executable repository evidence. This does not confer support on adjacent Haxe semantics or make a release promise.
statusDefinitions.scaffold-only: A typed contract, seed, fixture, or plan exists, but it is not evidence of an available user-program capability.
statusDefinitions.experimental: The surface is explicit and opt-in, remains unstable, and has not passed a supported-release capability gate.
statusDefinitions.unsupported: The surface is absent, deliberately fails closed, or lacks the evidence needed for a product claim.

compatibility.profiles: This manifest describes evidence only and does not change portable or metal semantics.
compatibility.runtimePolicy: This manifest selects no hxrt feature and does not change auto, minimal, or none resolution.
compatibility.environments: Native fixture evidence is not a supported environment or platform tuple; no support matrix is claimed.
compatibility.generatedC: Only the admitted primitive static-function and local fixed-array/span slice has generated-C evidence.
compatibility.publicAbi: The target-facing vocabulary is provisional and no generated public C ABI is stable or supported.

claimGates.reason: The current ledger is a planning seed and general standard-library calls remain unsupported.
Only a bounded primitive static-function and local fixed-array/span subset reaches generated C.
No versioned benchmark methodology, dashboard, or regression budget has passed.
Security, compatibility, stdlib, platform, release, and broad semantic gates remain open.
No Haxe export analyzer, generated public header, ABI manifest, or compatibility policy is complete.
Current native CI is component evidence, not the complete ADR 0007 environment/runtime/architecture/compiler tuple matrix.
No publishable, signed, reproducible, evidence-backed release exists.

doctor.remediation: Use the documented repository validation commands today. E8.T06 must make future human and JSON doctor output consume these capability IDs rather than maintaining a second status list.

capabilities.summary: The checked-in Beads plan validates and previews deterministically without a live database.
capabilities.scope: The plan contains stable epic/task keys, blocking edges, requirement coverage, and an idempotent materializer.
limitations: This is workflow infrastructure, not compiler or runtime behavior.

capabilities.summary: No Clang-backed binding generator or user bindgen command exists.
capabilities.scope: The PRD and Beads graph reserve deterministic parsing, mapping, locking, ABI probing, and wrapper work.
limitations: Independent native C fixtures are not generated bindings.

capabilities.summary: A schema-checked M0 capability and limitation inventory drives the README status table.
capabilities.scope: The manifest fixes status meanings, evidence paths, ownership, hard claim gates, and future doctor consumption.
limitations: It reports evidence and changes no compiler behavior.

capabilities.summary: Target-owned structural C11 declarator, expression, statement, literal, and escaping corpora compile and run.
capabilities.scope: Direct Haxe fixtures build typed C AST nodes and emit deterministic strict-C11 translation units.
limitations: Direct AST fixtures alone do not prove Haxe-language lowering.

capabilities.summary: No Haxe export analyzer, public-header generator, library packaging, or ABI diff workflow exists.
capabilities.scope: The Beads E7 plan owns exported declarations, ABI-safe types, wrappers, libraries, manifests, and consumers.
limitations: Independent native C/C++ consumers are boundary seeds, not generated export evidence.

capabilities.summary: The c23 internal dialect spelling is explicit and opt-in but has no support-matrix claim.
capabilities.scope: Configuration recognizes c23 while public headers remain constrained to the C11 contract.
limitations: It has no independent native syntax matrix and cannot change semantics or ABI.

capabilities.summary: Typed profile, runtime-policy, environment, and build-mode values exist as compiler contracts.
capabilities.scope: Direct defines resolve selected bootstrap facts, and build mode cannot weaken admitted span semantics.
limitations: There is no hxc.json parser, precedence model, schema, canonical effective-config report, or complete capability matrix.

capabilities.summary: Structural declaration planning handles complete types, forward declarations, includes, and private boundaries deterministically.
capabilities.scope: Test-owned adapters emit independently compilable strict-C11 header plans from typed contract snapshots.
limitations: The planner fixture is not a generated public ABI or general Haxe declaration emitter.

capabilities.summary: Thirteen typed diagnostic IDs and deterministic schema-1 records are registry- and drift-checked.
capabilities.scope: Current bootstrap, configuration, lowering, runtime, target-contract, and internal failures use the target-owned catalog.
limitations: The public hxc JSON event stream, command context, and native-tool attachments remain unimplemented.

capabilities.summary: No repository-owned product example directories or executable generated examples exist.
capabilities.scope: The E9 plan reserves hello, no-runtime, interop, shared-library, todo, and WebAssembly examples.
limitations: Focused compiler fixtures and independent native inputs must not be relabeled as product examples.

capabilities.summary: Eight evidence lanes and centrally owned deterministic snapshots have fail-closed policy checks.
capabilities.scope: Focused M0 suites map their exact runners and evidence without treating examples as implicit tests.
limitations: A registered lane proves only its declared artifact and assertion scope.

capabilities.summary: General Haxe programs are not supported and stop at the first unsupported typed construct.
capabilities.scope: Only the separately listed primitive static-function and local fixed-array/span subset is admitted.
limitations: Objects, instances, virtual/interface calls, closures, general generics, strings, general arrays, exceptions, and dynamic/reflection semantics remain outside the admitted slice.

capabilities.summary: Admitted projects use deterministic content-addressed artifacts and Reflaxe-owned stale-file handling.
capabilities.scope: The writer rejects path escapes, symlink escapes, unowned collisions, and stale files outside the prior manifest.
limitations: Only wholly admitted compiler graphs may reach this output boundary.

capabilities.summary: Contribution, disclosure, license, vendoring, provenance, and future release responsibilities are drift-checked.
capabilities.scope: The repository has a private reporting path, GPL-3.0-only source policy, and fail-closed third-party inventory checks.
limitations: Governance policy is not a completed security review or release implementation.

capabilities.summary: No Run.hx, hxc command router, project schema, template, or packaged executable exists.
capabilities.scope: Direct Haxe/HXML remains the only user-program compiler invocation; E8 owns the optional orchestration layer.
limitations: Repository Python validation scripts are developer tooling and are not hxc commands.

capabilities.summary: The hxc doctor human and JSON command is not implemented.
capabilities.scope: This manifest provides the capability IDs and current unsupported contract that E8.T06 must consume.
limitations: No tool, SDK, sysroot, compiler, sanitizer, or writable-output probe is exposed as hxc doctor today.

capabilities.summary: Schema-2 HxcIR structurally records values, places, ordering, control flow, failures, cleanup, and runtime intent.
capabilities.scope: The validator and deterministic dumper cover the semantic core consumed by the admitted primitive lowering slice.
limitations: An IR node does not imply frontend lowering or C emission exists for that semantic feature.

capabilities.summary: Independent C-library and C++ extern-C shim fixtures validate future interop boundary shapes.
capabilities.scope: The native inputs compile and run as isolated C/C++ consumer evidence.
limitations: They are not generated from Haxe and do not prove bindgen, export, or generated ABI support.

capabilities.summary: Strict GCC/G++ and Clang/Clang++ CI lanes compile and run the declared structural and primitive native corpus.
capabilities.scope: The matrix uses strict C11/C++17 flags and exercises the precise fixture set registered by the runner.
limitations: Component-native evidence is not a complete supported-platform or release matrix.

capabilities.summary: No compiler-time, C-compile-time, runtime, size, allocation, FFI, or agent benchmark claim is validated.
capabilities.scope: Future versioned methodology and budgets must precede performance or hand-written-quality marketing claims.
limitations: Code-shape tests and deterministic output are not performance benchmarks.

capabilities.summary: No operating-system, architecture, environment, runtime, and compiler tuple is currently a supported release lane.
capabilities.scope: ADR 0007 records the future Tier 1 gate; current local and CI compilers provide component evidence only.
limitations: Cross compilation or one native fixture cannot establish a supported platform tuple.

capabilities.summary: A bounded primitive static-function, deterministic initialization, and local fixed-array/span graph emits and runs runtime-free strict C11.
capabilities.scope: The slice covers typed constants, locals, globals, dependency-ordered class/static initialization, direct calls, conversions, sequenced control flow, UB-safe arithmetic, and admitted spans.
limitations: The scope is not general Haxe, standard-library parity, object semantics, or a hello product example.

capabilities.summary: A typed schema-2 primitive representation, nullability, conversion, and operation contract has independent C evidence.
capabilities.scope: Void, Bool, Int, UInt, Float, exact and ABI integers, and the registered operation slice preserve explicit semantics without hxrt.
limitations: The contract does not cover strings, objects, general arrays, dynamic values, or arbitrary native ABI layout facts.

capabilities.summary: No generated public C header, stable export symbol set, ownership boundary, or ABI compatibility promise exists.
capabilities.scope: E7 and E10 own generated headers, wrappers, manifests, independent consumers, and stabilization.
limitations: Private generated headers and target-facing c.* contracts are not a public ABI.

capabilities.summary: The Reflaxe adapter captures complete typed modules and routes admitted output through request-local compiler state.
capabilities.scope: Typed input is captured before callback filtering and compiler-server requests clear all pending/current storage.
limitations: Adapter activation does not mean every typed declaration or expression is lowerable.

capabilities.summary: No publishable compiler package, signed reproducible archive, supported version, or release automation exists.
capabilities.scope: Current package metadata and package-layout probes are development inputs only.
limitations: Generated-output and runtime redistribution treatment also remains a separate pre-release decision.

capabilities.summary: A typed deterministic feature graph resolves source-rooted closure and packages only selected provisional native-seed slices.
capabilities.scope: The production primitive compiler consumes the planner for positive empty plans; canonical native fixtures exercise exact alloc-only and string closures.
limitations: No hxrt feature is compiler-selectable yet, so runtime-requiring Haxe semantics still fail closed.
The provisional catalog and plan formats are not a stable public runtime ABI.

capabilities.summary: A provisional native allocator, string, status, and Int32 runtime ABI seed compiles independently.
capabilities.scope: Hosted/freestanding C, C++17 header consumption, and exact alloc-only/string packages validate only the split native seed itself.
limitations: Generated Haxe does not select or link this seed, and it is not an unconditional runtime core.

capabilities.summary: General Haxe standard-library parity is not implemented.
capabilities.scope: Only compiler-recognized primitive operations such as the admitted Std.int path are covered by their exact lowering tests.
limitations: String, collections, Bytes/I/O, general Std/Math/Type/Reflect, JSON, regex, time, sys, networking, and threading remain roadmap work.

capabilities.summary: The exact pinned Haxe public standard-library surface has a deterministic ownership and parity ledger.
capabilities.scope: Typed probes inventory 183 applicable source modules and 2,311 public APIs, then assign implementation, profile, environment, candidate runtime, diagnostics, Beads, and test ownership with an exact generated CSV review view.
limitations: This implements inventory and drift governance, not the APIs: only the exact Std.int row is currently conformant and broad standard-library behavior remains unsupported.

capabilities.summary: Per-compilation C namespace ownership and deterministic exact/generated symbol finalization are implemented.
capabilities.scope: The registry validates authored names, escapes reserved generated forms, handles overload/specialization identity, and records collisions.
limitations: Stable export ABI names remain unratified and broader typed-AST symbol collection is incomplete.

capabilities.summary: The pinned Haxe 5 CustomTarget(c) carrier configures scalar Unicode facts and exactly-once target registration.
capabilities.scope: Cold, package, compiler-server, non-C isolation, conflict, legacy Cross, and fail-closed production paths are tested.
limitations: Successful activation establishes target typing facts, not general lowering or platform support.

capabilities.summary: Typed c.* pointer, span, ownership, integer, layout, linkage, and metadata contracts type-check.
capabilities.scope: The contract macro validates the current declaration/build surface; fixed CArray and local Span operations have a separate admitted lowering slice.
limitations: Most c.* operations have no runtime body or compiler lowering, and Syntax/Unsafe intentionally expose no authority.

capabilities.summary: No Haxe threading, synchronization, TLS, or atomic capability is advertised or implemented.
capabilities.scope: Target setup deliberately leaves target.threaded and target.atomics absent until an adapter proves them.
limitations: The presence of C11 atomic AST nodes or c.Atomic contracts is not Haxe concurrency support.

capabilities.summary: Haxe 5.0.0-preview.1, Reflaxe, Lix, package metadata, checksums, and provenance are pinned and verified.
capabilities.scope: Fresh package resolution and installed/source-checkout lifecycle probes use the reviewed dependency contract.
limitations: Pinned development dependencies are not a packaged or supported compiler release.

capabilities.summary: Complete typed-module capture and deterministic normalized ownership survive order changes and compiler-server reuse.
capabilities.scope: The adapter retains raw compiler objects while serializing only path-stable module, declaration, metadata, field, expression-root, and entry facts.
limitations: Typed-input capture is not semantic lowering, and unsupported nodes still fail with HXC1001.

capabilities.summary: Repository-owned Haxe has a source-exact guard against unreviewed untyped compiler seams.
capabilities.scope: The inventory scans Dynamic, Any, Reflect, untyped, open-ended carriers, casts, private access, raw injection, and JSON ingress while requiring closed typed models and negative evidence for each exception.
limitations: This protects compiler implementation technique; it does not implement user-language Dynamic or reflection semantics.

capabilities.summary: Namespaced typed-C declaration and build metadata produce a deterministic validated structural snapshot.
capabilities.scope: The macro rejects unknown metadata, invalid literals, duplicate names, unsafe paths, opaque-by-value use, cycles, and unsupported static assertions.
limitations: A valid contract snapshot does not itself emit a public header, select runtime code, or prove native layout.
